rationale: A suspiciously named executable from the Downloads folder ('ILL_UNBLINKING.exe', EV-001/EV-002/EV-004) repeatedly spawns notepad.exe instances and immediately injects remote threads into each one (A0, EV-003, EV-005) — a classic process injection pattern used to execute shellcode under a trusted host process. The injected thread start addresses resolve to no known module ('-'), indicating shellcode mapped directly into the target process's memory rather than a legitimate DLL, which rules out benign inter-process activity. The pattern repeats three times within ~3 minutes, suggesting an automated injection loop. The executable's location (Downloads), non-standard name, and lack of any signed-binary or vendor context further eliminate legitimate software explanations.
